title: Surface knowledge record
description: Durable, accumulating, two-sided (old/new) record of a surface's components, their data sources, and the classified old->new gaps. The record is a Markdown file: this structured object is carried in its YAML **front-matter** (the leading `---` block), and the prose body below it holds the human-readable narrative (per-component `whatItDoes`/`behavior`, per-gap `oldState`/`newState`) — those narrative fields are optional in the front-matter and not machine-validated. Built by gather-surface-knowledge.md; consumed by write-story / plan / implement / QA. features/ is hand-curated and never written here. Legacy raw `.json` records still validate for back-compat.

chromeContext.description: Context-conditional chrome: where this element is present vs. absent (e.g. a project picker shown outside a project but hidden inside one). Lets the author plan a presence/absence AC per context instead of catching it by luck at QA.

feedbackKind.description: `transient` ⇒ feedback that appears then clears (save flash/toast, inline validation, optimistic UI) — the author plans an appear-then-disappear AC. `steady` ⇒ persistent state.

disposition.description: How this gap is handled relative to the authoring story. Absent ⇒ treated as `scoped` (this story closes it). `deferred` ⇒ a later owner closes it and `owner` MUST name that owner. `dropped` ⇒ intentionally not built (record the decision in newState).

owner.description: Required iff disposition == 'deferred'. A resolvable owner of the deferred work: an existing story slug, an open backlog item id (the bare id, e.g. 'project-editor-section-tree-blank'), or a seed-item id of an already-authored epic.

journeys.description: Documented typical user journeys for this surface, read from the repo's feature docs. Each becomes a journey-level acceptance criterion the writer must include.

chromeContext.description: Context-conditional chrome: where this component is present vs. absent.
